claim: The absorption-transformation coding may naturalize the continuity of British power in post-imperial structures, obscuring that the Commonwealth preserved British economic and diplomatic dominance under a transformed symbolic vocabulary rather than achieving genuine transformation of the underlying order.
rationale: Scholars of postcolonial continuity argue that the Commonwealth transition reproduced structural dependencies under a new symbolic frame — what appears as transformation of the symbolic order may be better coded as institutional self-preservation with symbolic rebranding.
